response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to a roughly six-month stint at National Instruments in 2007-2008 plus hobby projects, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His career since has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MySQL/MongoDB only), and no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets. The gap on every mandatory criterion makes an interview hard to justify.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Solid CI/CD, scripting, and testing-infrastructure background matching the desirable skills list
concerns: Effectively no recent or substantial professional C++ experience, and none on Windows
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain knowledge
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No capital-markets or fixed-income domain exposure
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems fundamentals
